2b1ab06299308066acc9dcba920db5a0.title: Moravian - Checkpoint 1 - Due 11/30/25
2b1ab06299308066acc9dcba920db5a0.characteristics: By Nov 30, EAB must agree to the FY24-25 SPSI budget projections, volume projections, and cost-per-student model that Rhodes Advisors and SPSI leadership are finalizing. This will serve as the financial baseline for measuring performance and pacing. All SPSI programs fully reactivated across paid and organic journeys. Initial improvements to attribution and source tracking.

2b1ab06299308083b020ffce51a097d9.title: Moravian - Checkpoint 2 - Due 1/28/26
2b1ab06299308083b020ffce51a097d9.characteristics: January 28: Full implementation of Rhodes Advisors-provided creative, landing pages, and messaging. CRM workflows updated to match SPSI’s journey mapping. Tracking alignment across EAB and the secondary vendor to enable February funnel clarity.

2b1ab062993080bea721de915fa19782.title: Moravian - Checkpoint 3 - Due 2/26/26
2b1ab062993080bea721de915fa19782.characteristics: By February 26: Clear progress in lead volume, CPL, and early conversion quality. Positive pacing toward Fall 2025 enrollment targets. Weekly program-level funnel reporting begins. February becomes the first unified view of lead → application → student performance. If progress is insufficient, Moravian begins drafting vendor transition contracts for a September 1 takeover or earlier if necessary.

2b1ab062993080bcb96bd9d5871956bc.title: Moravian - Post February Oversight - Ongoing
2b1ab062993080bcb96bd9d5871956bc.characteristics: Post-February Oversight: Weekly Performance Reviews — Rhodes Advisors and Moravian will jointly review SPSI performance every week, focused on lead volume, CPL, conversion quality, and pacing toward Fall 2025 goals. Financial Stewardship — All performance evaluations measured against the SPSI budget, volume projections, and cost-per-student model agreed to by EAB and shared with Moravian leadership. Pipeline Health Monitoring — SPSI must demonstrate consistent inquiry growth, improved lead quality, and stable funnel movement. Fail-Safe Protocol — If progress stalls, Moravian will escalate toward transition planning with vendor contracts drafted in parallel. Clear Ownership Model — Justine Bartholomew (institutional lead) and Rhodes Advisors (strategic/performance lead) jointly own EAB coordination and KPI management.

2b1ab06299308040a986fb71a7b5dcfb.title: Moravian - Executive Rec Memo (EAB)
2b1ab06299308040a986fb71a7b5dcfb.characteristics: Email Lee sent to SLT (Bryon, Dee, Scott) wrapping up the full assessment of EAB in relation to SPSI marketing and enrollment operations. Provides a clear, evidence-based plan with a checkpoint model giving Moravian the structure, pace, and accountability to monitor EAB alignment while preserving SPSI agility if performance does not improve.

2a3ab06299308050aa70e16a1e8c4412.characteristics: Improve workflows for admissions processing, data sync, and lead handling. Train teams on KPI review process. Introduce system audits and data quality checks. Outcomes: updated process documentation, audit logs, team training materials.

2a3ab062993080e78a79c16f0387ecbe.title: Moravian - M1: Student Journey Mapping & Optimization
2a3ab062993080e78a79c16f0387ecbe.characteristics: Audit our new end-to-end journey (Inquiry → Enrollment → Onboarding). Identify friction points, response delays, and content gaps. Develop updated journey model with optimized touchpoints. Outcomes: student journey map, optimization brief, KPI alignment for each stage.

2a3ab06299308067b76fd32d5c46b0d4.title: Moravian - M2: Program Discovery Synthesis Report
2a3ab06299308067b76fd32d5c46b0d4.characteristics: Conduct discovery sessions with program directors (MBA, Cybersecurity, Well-Being Psychology, BBA). Capture program differentiators, audience insights, and competitive positioning. Review external market data (Lightcast, Environmental Scan) to identify value propositions and competitive whitespace. Document alignment opportunities between program strengths and regional workforce trends. Outcome: synthesis reports.

2a3ab062993080d5a36fe4312e0fc1ed.title: Moravian - M3: Messaging Framework & GTM Foundation

Identify shared themes (ROI, flexibility, industry relevance) and program-specific differentiators. Begin creative brief and campaign architecture outlining key audiences and tone of voice. Align early messaging with comm flow structure (awareness → inquiry). Outcomes: SPSI messaging framework and narrative hierarchy, draft creative brief and tone-of-voice guide, updated content map linking programs to audience segments, executive level presentation.

2a3ab0629930803cb96cdbee3e42ccb2.title: Moravian - M4: Creative Strategy & Media Plan
2a3ab0629930803cb96cdbee3e42ccb2.characteristics: Translate messaging framework into creative strategy (ad concepts, landing pages, web updates). Develop media plan (paid, owned, and earned channels) with spend allocation by program and audience. Align creative sequencing to funnel stages (top-of-funnel awareness → inquiry capture). Coordinate with EAB on channel readiness, performance expectations, and tracking setup. Outcomes: creative and campaign framework, media plan and channel sequencing strategy, launch readiness checklist.

2a3ab062993080a68b55c905191144e2.title: Moravian - M5: Campaign Prep & Early Activation
2a3ab062993080a68b55c905191144e2.characteristics: Tasks in scope (all programs): Meta ads — Instagram & Facebook creative (all required image sizes); Landing page designs — high-fidelity designs ready for build; Email sequence assets — HTML and hi-fi designs for 7 emails tied to student journey map / communications map; Website copy — new copy for SPSI, MBA, BBA, and Psych matching current templates; Mock-up of lead form — high-fidelity design aligned to campaign strategy and CRM requirements.

2a3ab0629930804983aec08088ae3c95.title: Moravian - M6: Full Launch & Optimization Readiness
2a3ab0629930804983aec08088ae3c95.characteristics: Fully activate top-of-funnel campaigns across all paid and organic channels. Prepare optimization brief for next phase (conversion-stage creative and communications). Outcomes: top-of-funnel campaigns live (4 programs + SPSI brand), initial performance report and KPI dashboard connection, optimization and next-phase plan.

2a3ab06299308017aef5df570bbc1078.title: Moravian - M1: Input Validation & Market Alignment
2a3ab06299308017aef5df570bbc1078.characteristics: Work with Anna to align the financial model with live marketing and enrollment realities. Reconcile initial projections with verified cost-per-lead (CPL), cost-per-application (CPA), and conversion rate benchmarks emerging from marketing discovery. Audit tuition, discount, and retention assumptions to reflect updated program data and student behavior patterns. Identify deltas between prior growth assumptions and achievable marketing performance. Outcomes: updated financial model inputs reflecting verified market data, reconciliation report, summary of enrollment and revenue alignment with marketing projections.

2a3ab06299308095a3b0f99efa37be1d.title: Moravian - M2: Model Refinement & Scenario Development
2a3ab06299308095a3b0f99efa37be1d.characteristics: Incorporate verified marketing performance metrics (lead flow, cost efficiency, yield) into model scenarios. Create Baseline, Target, and Stretch models showing outcomes under varied spend and conversion conditions. Conduct sensitivity testing to identify inflection points for spend vs. yield efficiency. Establish cross-functional review cadence between marketing, finance, and enrollment. Outcomes: rebuilt financial model with market-informed assumptions, scenario testing outputs (Baseline/Target/Stretch), leadership review materials linking marketing data to revenue forecasts.

2a3ab0629930806db023cd9b2260a564.title: Moravian - M3: KPI Definition & Reporting Framework

Establish performance cadence for weekly and monthly reporting cycles. Outcomes: KPI definition document and ownership map, integrated data alignment plan, initial KPI dashboard prototype and reporting cadence.

330ab062993080e0884eebd3145f1121.title: MS Cybersecurity Program Viability Assessment -Packet
330ab062993080e0884eebd3145f1121.characteristics: Project: MS Cybersecurity Program Viability Assessment Objective Produce a concise, data-backed go/no-go recommendation on the MS in Cybersecurity program by evaluating market demand, enrollment feasibility, and competitive positioning in Moravian's target geography. Primary Research Questions ● Is there sufficient, quantifiable demand for a master's-level cybersecurity program in the Greater Bethlehem / Eastern Pennsylvania region? ● Is the program's competitive environment navigable — is there a realistic, defensible path to enrollment growth? ● Is current enrollment underperformance more likely attributable to a market demand problem or an execution and positioning problem? ● Based on the evidence, what is the recommended path forward: continue, continue with defined changes, or discontinue? Scope of Work Demand Analysis Quantify current and near-term employer demand for cybersecurity talent in the target region using labor market data. Assess degree-level requirements versus alternative credentials (bootcamps, certifications) among employers and prospective students. Identify the size and accessibility of the addressable student population. Enrollment Feasibility Modeling Using available market data and RA benchmarks, develop a directional enrollment model estimating realistic cohort potential. Identify the primary friction points — demand volume, price sensitivity, modality, competitive alternatives — that most directly constrain enrollment performance. Competitive Landscape Assessment Evaluate 5–8 directly competing master's-level cybersecurity programs on dimensions including enrollment model, pricing, modality, target audience, and positioning. Assess degree of market saturation and identify whether meaningful differentiation is achievable. Performance Gap Diagnosis Using market data and available program performance indicators, assess whether current enrollment gaps are more consistent with a structural demand problem or a correctable execution and positioning problem. This distinction is central to the go/no-go recommendation. Go / No-Go Recommendation Synthesize findings into a clear, evidence-based recommendation: ● Go — market conditions support continued investment ● Go with Conditions — viable with specific, defined changes to audience, positioning, or program structure ● No-Go — market evidence does not support continued investment at this time Each recommendation will be supported by the quantitative and qualitative evidence gathered across the scope above. Deliverable A concise written analysis report including: ● Demand landscape summary with supporting data ● Enrollment feasibility assessment with directional modeling ● Competitive landscape summary ● Performance gap diagnosis ● Go / No-Go recommendation with supporting rationale ● If Go with Conditions: specific, actionable conditions defined The format will be a tight written memo or brief deck — whichever Micah determines best serves clarity and speed given the timeline. Completeness of evidence takes priority over format.

33aab0629930807da2d7eb6c9fb65108.title: 30Credit MBA Financial Analysis
33aab0629930807da2d7eb6c9fb65108.characteristics: Rhodes Advisors was engaged to evaluate Moravian University's proposed restructuring of its MBA program from 36 to 30 credits, with a specific focus on assessing the financial sustainability and market viability of revised tuition price points at $21,000, $25,000, and $30,000. The goal of this analysis is to quantify the revenue and enrollment implications of the credit reduction, determine the enrollment thresholds required to offset per-student revenue loss, and assess whether each pricing scenario is defensible within the competitive market—ultimately informing a data-driven recommendation on optimal program pricing and positioning.
